title: Rustic Roasted Croatian Pork Knuckle Roast
description: This earthy and crunchy dish features a pork knuckle roast with a fermented twist, making it an adventurous Croatian meal without onions or garlic.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together caraway seeds, coriander seeds, and juniper berries.
Rub the mixture all over the pork knuckle, making sure to get some under the skin as well.
In a large bowl, whisk together apple cider vinegar, water, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pork knuckle to the bowl and let it marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the pork knuckle from the marinade and pat it dry with paper towels.
Heat the lard or vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork knuckle until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and roast the pork knuckle for 2 hours.
Increase the oven temperature to 400 degrees Fahrenheit and continue roasting for an additional 30 minutes, or until the skin is crispy.
